title: Richardson 112 Trucker Cap: The Go-To Custom Hat
metaDescription: The Richardson 112 is the most-requested custom trucker cap for PNW crews. Explore colorways, fit, and the 112FP, PM, PL, PT family. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
Walk onto any job site, brewery patio, or company golf tournament in the Puget Sound and you'll spot it: the Richardson 112. It's the trucker cap that crews reach for again and again, and it's the single most-requested custom hat that comes through our shop here in Milton. There's a reason for that. The 112 nails the classic snapback trucker look, holds embroidery and leather patches beautifully, and comes in a colorway lineup deep enough to match almost any brand. If you're deciding on a hat to put your logo on, this is the one to start with.

## Why the 112 Is the PNW Crew Favorite

The [Richardson 112](/custom-richardson) is a structured, mid-profile trucker with a mesh back and a plastic snapback closure. That combination is what makes it work for so many different customers. The structured front panel stands up straight, giving your logo a clean, flat canvas instead of slumping the way an unstructured cap does. The mesh back keeps it breathable for outdoor crews putting in long summer days, and the snapback means one size genuinely fits most heads on the team, so you're not sorting through fitted sizes at handout time.

For Pacific Northwest companies, that versatility matters. The same hat lands right on a landscaping crew, a coffee roaster, a construction outfit, a craft distillery, and a youth sports club. It reads casual and rugged without looking cheap, and it photographs well for social media and team pages. When a business calls us asking for "that trucker hat everyone has," they almost always mean the [112](/product.html?style=112).

## The 112 Family: Pick Your Look

The classic 112 is just the start. Richardson builds a whole family around the same trusted trucker silhouette, so you can keep the shape your crew loves while changing up the construction or pattern. Here are the styles worth knowing:

- **112** — The original six-panel adjustable snapback trucker. Structured mid-profile, mesh back, and the widest solid and split colorway range. This is the safe, everybody-happy default.
- **112FP** — A five-panel version with a seamless, no-center-seam front. That single smooth panel gives you the cleanest possible canvas for one logo, which is why it's a favorite for larger front designs and printing.
- **112PM** — The printed-mesh member of the family. The pattern — camo, fade, or stars-and-stripes — carries across the mesh back, not just the front, for an all-over outdoor look.
- **112PL** — The flexible-fit option (Richardson's R-Flex). It adds an elastic sweatband and stretch mesh for a more forgiving, comfortable fit while keeping the familiar trucker profile.
- **112PT** — The tactical-leaning member, built around military-inspired printed patterns like Multicam and arid tones for rugged, outdoor, and duty-style brands.

If you're not sure which variation fits your brand, the plain 112 is almost always the right first pick. Reach for the 112FP when you want the cleanest single-panel front for a bigger logo, the 112PM or 112PT when you want an outdoorsy or tactical printed pattern, and the 112PL when a more flexible, comfortable fit matters most to your crew.

## Embroidery or Leather Patch: Your Call

However you want it decorated, the 112 delivers. We run flat and 3D-puff embroidery directly on the front panel for a sharp, durable stitch that holds up wash after wash. That's the go-to for clean logos, monograms, and lettering.

For that heritage, outdoor-brand feel, we also do leather and faux-leather patches on the 112. A laser-engraved or debossed patch stitched to the front gives your hat that premium, small-batch look you see on the best craft and trades brands. It's especially popular with breweries, coffee shops, contractors, and anyone wanting their cap to feel like merch people actually want to wear off the clock.

Because the whole family shares the same trucker fit, the logo setup we build for your classic 112 generally carries right over to the 112FP, PM, PL, or PT if you decide to run a few different looks for the same crew.

## Get Your Crew Capped

The Richardson 112 earns its spot as our most-requested custom trucker for a simple reason: it works for nearly everyone, and it makes your logo look good doing it. Whether you want crisp embroidery or a rugged leather patch, whether you go classic solid or printed pattern, we'll help you land on the right version for your team.

Ready to outfit your crew? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and tell us your style and decoration, or browse the full [Richardson lineup](/custom-richardson) to compare colorways. We've been decorating apparel for Pacific Northwest companies out of Milton since 1977, and we'd love to put your logo on the hat everyone actually wears.

title: Best Carhartt Styles for Custom Company Workwear
metaDescription: The Carhartt jackets, hoodies, sweatshirts, and vests PNW crews order for branded workwear that lasts. See top styles and get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
When a crew wants gear that survives real work and still looks sharp with a logo on the chest, they ask for Carhartt. Here in Milton, WA we outfit construction crews, contractors, landscapers, and shop teams across the Pacific Northwest, and Carhartt is the name that keeps coming back. It's built for weather and wear, it holds a clean embroidered logo, and it earns respect on a jobsite the way few brands can. Below are the pieces our customers actually order for branded company workwear, and who each one suits.

## Why Carhartt Is the Premium Workwear Pick

Carhartt is heavyweight, honest gear. The fabrics are thick, the seams are reinforced where it matters, and the fits are cut for people who move and lift all day. That durability is exactly why it takes decoration so well: rugged cotton and canvas give embroidery a firm, stable base, so your company logo comes out crisp and stays put through wash after wash. For a business, that means uniforms that still look professional a year in, not faded giveaways you replace every season.

It's also a brand your team is proud to wear off the clock, which quietly turns every jacket and hoodie into rolling advertising around town. Browse the full lineup on our [custom Carhartt page](/custom-carhartt) to see what fits your crew.

## The Carhartt Styles Crews Order Most

Here are the workhorses, sorted by what job they do best:

- **[Carhartt Duck Jacket (CT104670)](/product.html?style=CT104670)** — The flagship outerwear piece. Rugged duck canvas built to take abuse outdoors, so it's the go-to for construction, utility, and field crews who need a durable branded jacket that reads "serious company."
- **[Carhartt Midweight Hooded Sweatshirt (CT100617)](/product.html?style=CT100617)** — The everyday layer. A hood, roomy fit, and warm fleece make this the piece guys grab every morning. Perfect for a company hoodie that gets worn on and off the job.
- **[Carhartt Crewneck Sweatshirt (CTK121)](/product.html?style=CTK121)** — A classic, no-hood option for teams that want a cleaner look. Great for shop staff, office-plus-warehouse crews, and anyone who prefers a simple, sturdy pullover under a vest.
- **[Carhartt Gilliam Vest (CT102208)](/product.html?style=CT102208)** — Core warmth without the sleeves. A lined vest is ideal for supervisors, sales-and-site hybrids, and layering over a hoodie when the mornings are cold but the work runs hot.
- **[Carhartt CT100615](/product.html?style=CT100615)** — Another crew favorite that rounds out a coordinated uniform program, giving you a matching piece to pair with the jacket and hoodie for a head-to-toe branded look.

## Building a Branded Program That Works

Most companies don't buy just one piece — they build a small system. A common setup: the Duck Jacket for winter and field days, the midweight hoodie as the daily layer, the Gilliam Vest for the in-between mornings, and a crewneck or CT100615 for the guys who like it simple. Put the same logo on all of them and you've got a coordinated look across every role and season.

A few things we help crews get right:

- **Logo placement** — Left-chest embroidery is the standard for a clean, professional company look; back or sleeve placements work well when you want visibility from a distance.
- **Color consistency** — Sticking to one or two garment colors keeps the team looking uniform in photos and on site.
- **Sizing across a crew** — Carhartt runs roomy and true to its work heritage; we'll help you spec a size run that fits everyone from the new hire to the foreman.

Because we do embroidery in house right here in Milton, we control the quality from stitch one, and we can keep your logo file on record so reorders stay identical as your team grows.

## Ready to Outfit Your Crew?

Carhartt is the premium, durable choice when you want workwear that lasts and represents your company well. Whether you're kitting out a five-person shop or a full field crew, we'll help you pick the right pieces, nail the logo, and keep every reorder consistent. [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and tell us your styles, colors, and headcount — we'll take it from there.

title: CornerStone Hi-Vis: Best ANSI Safety Styles for Custom Workwear
metaDescription: The CornerStone hi-vis vests and shirts PNW crews order for custom branded workwear, plus when ANSI/ISEA 107 certification actually matters.
bodyMarkdown:
If your crew works near traffic, heavy equipment, or a busy yard, high-visibility gear is not optional — and it looks a lot better with your company name on it. CornerStone builds a deep line of hi-vis workwear that decorates cleanly, wears hard, and hits the price point crews actually reorder. At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we outfit road crews, landscapers, warehouse teams, and construction outfits all over the Puget Sound, and CornerStone is one of the brands we reach for again and again.

Before you pick, one thing matters more than color: **certification**. Some jobs require ANSI/ISEA 107 certified garments. Others just want the team to stand out. Below are the CornerStone styles we recommend most, and where each one fits.

## When ANSI/ISEA 107 certification actually matters

If a job site, DOT contract, or safety officer requires certified high-visibility apparel, you need a garment that carries an **ANSI/ISEA 107 Class** rating — Class 2 or Class 3 for most roadside and high-traffic work. A certified vest is engineered and tested as a complete safety garment: background fabric, reflective tape placement, and coverage all count.

Here's the compliance point we never blur: a **certified vest is a certified garment**. A screen-printed or embroidered hi-vis *tee* is highly visible and great for team identity, but the shirt itself is not a certified safety garment unless it's specifically built and labeled to an ANSI class. When decoration covers reflective or background material, it can also affect a garment's rating. So if your site requires certification, spec a certified vest — and add branded hi-vis shirts as a complementary layer, not a replacement. When in doubt, ask us and we'll match the right piece to your requirement.

## CornerStone hi-vis styles crews order

- **CSV105 — Class 2 Mesh Safety Vest.** Our go-to when the job requires certification. ANSI/ISEA 107 Class 2, breathable mesh, and roomy enough to layer over a jacket. Front and back decoration space for a company name and logo.
- **CS410 — Hi-Vis Tee.** A safety-yellow/orange short-sleeve tee that keeps a team visible and looking uniform. Great for warmer days and as a base layer under a vest.
- **CS413 — Hi-Vis Pocket Tee.** Same visibility, with a chest pocket for the crews who want somewhere to stash a pen or badge. Popular with supervisors.
- **CS409 — Hi-Vis Long Sleeve Tee.** The cooler-morning pick for Pacific Northwest crews — full-coverage visibility without jumping to a jacket.
- **CS412 — Hi-Vis Performance Tee.** Moisture-wicking knit for crews moving all day. Keeps color bright and the wearer drier.
- **CS430 — Safety Colors Tee.** A bold safety-color tee for teams that want the high-visibility *look* and strong branding. Note: this is a standout color option, **not** a certified ANSI garment — choose it for identity and events, not for jobs that require certification.

Every one of these takes embroidery or print well. For hi-vis tees, we generally recommend keeping logos clear of any reflective elements and sizing artwork so your brand reads from a distance.

## Build a program, not just a one-off order

Most crews we work with end up mixing pieces: certified CSV105 vests for the regulated work, plus branded CS410/CS413 tees for daily wear and team identity. That combination keeps everyone compliant and consistent. If you want reflective striping, a sewn-in logo, or a coordinated look across vests and shirts, take a look at our [custom safety apparel and safety-stripe program](/custom-safety-apparel) — it's built for exactly this. You can browse the full CornerStone line on our [CornerStone brand page](/custom-cornerstone), or jump straight to a style like the [CSV105 vest](/product.html?style=CSV105), [CS410 tee](/product.html?style=CS410), or [CS413 pocket tee](/product.html?style=CS413).

Not sure which class you need, or how to split your order between certified vests and branded shirts? That's what we're here for. Tell us about your crew and your job site, and we'll spec the right pieces and get artwork rolling. [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and a real person in Milton will help you build a hi-vis program that keeps your team safe, visible, and unmistakably yours.

title: Best Custom Polos for Uniforms: Sport-Tek vs Port Authority vs Nike
metaDescription: Compare Sport-Tek ST650, Port Authority K500, and Nike Dri-FIT custom polos for company uniforms. Find the right fit for your crew and get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
Picking a custom polo for company uniforms comes down to one question: what does your crew actually do all day? A polo that's perfect for a landscaping team sweating through a July afternoon is the wrong call for a front-desk staff greeting clients, and neither is quite right when you want a premium, name-brand look for account managers in the field. At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've embroidered thousands of uniform polos for Pacific Northwest crews and companies, and three lines come up again and again: Sport-Tek, Port Authority, and Nike. Here's how they compare on feel, care, and branding — and who each one fits.

## Sport-Tek ST650: The Performance Pick

The [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) ST650 Micropique Sport-Wick is built for teams that move. It's a moisture-wicking performance polo with a smooth micropique face, so it sheds sweat, dries fast, and keeps its shape through a long shift. If your people are on their feet — warehouse crews, event staff, delivery drivers, coaches, and anyone working outdoors through a PNW summer — this is the one that earns its keep.

- **[Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650)** — Moisture-wicking micropique that stays cool and dry; the go-to for active, on-their-feet teams.
- Snag-resistant knit holds up to daily wear and frequent washing without looking tired.
- A wide color range and a clean athletic cut make it easy to match brand colors across a whole crew.

Embroidery sits crisp on the ST650's tight knit face, so your logo reads sharp even after repeated washing. For any company that wants a uniform to look like a uniform — coordinated, durable, and comfortable in the heat — this is our default recommendation.

## Port Authority K500: The Office & Uniform Staple

The [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) K500 Silk Touch is the polo you've probably worn without knowing the style number. It's a soft, easy-care cotton-blend pique that's become the default for office staff, retail floors, hospitality, and service teams. The blend resists wrinkles and holds color wash after wash, which is exactly what you want when a uniform has to look presentable every single morning with zero fuss.

- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — Soft easy-care pique that stays professional shift after shift; the reliable office and uniform standard.
- Relaxed, roomy fit that flatters a wide range of body types across a mixed team.
- Deep color selection makes it simple to keep every department on the same page.

Where the ST650 leans athletic, the K500 leans classic and comfortable. It's the safe, smart choice when you're outfitting a broad group and want one polo that works for everyone from the front desk to the shop floor.

## Nike Dri-FIT: The Premium Brand Statement

Sometimes the logo on the chest isn't the only branding that matters — the swoosh does work too. [Nike](/custom-nike) Dri-FIT polos bring instant brand recognition and a premium feel that clients notice. For client-facing roles, sales teams, leadership, and companies that use their apparel as part of the impression they make, a Nike polo signals that you don't cut corners.

- **Nike Dri-FIT polos** — Premium moisture-wicking performance with a name customers recognize; ideal for client-facing and leadership teams.
- The Nike name adds perceived value that a house-brand polo can't match — useful for golf outings, trade shows, and executive gear.
- Performance fabric means it looks sharp and stays comfortable, so it works as both a uniform and a gift.

Nike is the step-up option when appearance and brand association are part of the goal. Many companies mix and match: K500s or ST650s for the broader crew, Nike polos for the folks who represent the company face-to-face.

## Which Polo Fits Your Crew?

Here's the short version. Choose the **ST650** for active, on-their-feet teams that need moisture-wicking performance. Choose the **K500** for office, retail, and general uniforms that need to look professional with easy care. Choose **Nike Dri-FIT** when brand recognition and a premium look matter most. Plenty of PNW companies run more than one across different roles — and we're happy to help you spec the mix.

Ready to outfit your team? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and tell us your styles, colors, and headcount. We'll handle the embroidery in-house right here in Milton and get your crew looking sharp.

title: Best Custom Hoodies for Winter Crews: Premium to Budget
metaDescription: The custom hoodies PNW crews order for cold-weather branded gear — Carhartt, Port & Company, Gildan. Screen-print & embroidery ready. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
When the Puget Sound damp sets in, a hoodie stops being a nice-to-have and becomes the layer your crew actually lives in. Around Milton and the greater Seattle-Tacoma area, we watch construction outfits, warehouse teams, brewery staff, and youth sports groups all reach for the same thing come October: a warm, durable pullover with the company mark on it. The trick is matching the hoodie to the job and the budget — a landscaping crew that beats up gear all week has different needs than a startup ordering swag for 20 people.

Here's how we'd break down the winter hoodie lineup, from premium down to budget, so any crew and any budget finds a fit. Every one of these is a proven blank for both screen print and embroidery.

## The tiers, and who each one is for

Think about three things before you pick: how hard the garment will get used, whether you want a plush hand-feel or a workhorse, and how the logo will be decorated. Heavier fleece and structured builds hold up to daily abuse and take embroidery beautifully. Lighter, budget-friendly blends are perfect for one-time events, giveaways, and big bulk runs where the count matters more than the fabric.

- **[Carhartt CT100617](/custom-carhartt) — Premium.** A midweight hooded sweatshirt built to Carhartt's work-tested standard. Rugged, warm, and it wears the brand name that trades and clients already respect. This is the one for crews who want gear that lasts seasons and looks sharp with an embroidered left chest.
- **[Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H) — Value workhorse.** The Essential Fleece pullover is the do-everything pick: a heavyweight fleece with a soft inside, a huge color range, and a price that scales well for medium-to-large orders. It's our most-ordered hoodie for good reason — it prints clean and embroiders well.
- **[Gildan 18500](/product.html?style=18500) — Budget bulk.** The Heavy Blend hooded sweatshirt is the go-to when you need a lot of warm, branded pullovers without stretching the budget. Great for events, fundraisers, school groups, and giveaways where quantity leads the decision.

## Premium: Carhartt CT100617

If your crew is out in the weather or in front of customers, the [Carhartt CT100617](/custom-carhartt) earns its keep. It's a midweight hooded sweatshirt with the durability Carhartt is known for, and the label itself does marketing work — a Carhartt hoodie reads as serious, quality gear before anyone even sees your logo. It's an excellent embroidery canvas; a stitched left-chest mark on Carhartt looks premium and holds up wash after wash. For contractors, trades, and any company that wants its branded layer to signal quality, this is the top of the roundup.

## Value and budget: PC90H and 18500

The [Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H) is where most crews land. It's a heavyweight fleece that feels substantial, comes in a deep color selection to match any brand palette, and takes both screen print and embroidery cleanly. For a company hoodie your team will actually wear all winter — without a premium price tag — it's hard to beat.

When the count climbs and the budget is the boss, the [Gildan 18500](/product.html?style=18500) Heavy Blend is the answer. It's warm, it's dependable, and it comes in at a price that lets you outfit a big group or hand out branded pullovers at an event without overthinking it. Screen printing shines here — bold, high-contrast logos on a budget blank are exactly what this hoodie was made for. Planning a large one-color or two-color run? Start with our [screen print quote builder](/quote-builders/screenprint-quote-builder.html).

## Get your crew sorted before the cold hits

Whichever tier fits, order early — winter branded gear moves fast once the temperature drops, and lead times tighten as everyone else has the same idea. Tell us your quantity, your decoration (embroidery or screen print), and your rough budget, and we'll steer you to the right hoodie and get you a number. We've been decorating apparel for Pacific Northwest crews and companies out of Milton, WA since 1977 — we'll make it easy. [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll take it from there.
